changes.check: 2A — Source cross-ref: no analytics SDK present
changes.evidence: Grep across frontend/app/ for posthog, mixpanel, segment, amplitude, ga4, analytics, plausible, track(), gtag (case-insensitive): zero matches in application code. analysisStore.ts confirmed: only console.log/debug/warn at lines 129, 130, 133, 176, 182, 183, 187, 200, etc. — no structured telemetry, no track() calls. Prompt Stage 1C §3 assertion 'no dedicated product analytics SDK/pipeline is present' CONFIRMED.

changes.check: 2A — Source cross-ref: clinician report export/share semantics (MATERIAL)
changes.evidence: frontend/app/(app)/results/page.tsx line 185: handleExportResults() — downloads currentAnalysis as JSON file named healthiq-analysis-{date}.json. This is a full analysis data export, NOT a clinician report PDF. Line 199: handleShareResults() — navigator.share with page URL, fallback to clipboard.writeText(location.href). This is a URL-share action, not a report-specific download. frontend/app/services/reports.ts line 11: API_BASE_URL = 'about:blank'; line 9 comment: 'Disabled: backend reports routes are not registered; keep file for future restoration'. ReportsService.downloadReport() is completely non-functional in current product. The 'clinician report viewed' event maps to ClinicianReportRenderer tab render (in-app). There is NO live server-side clinician PDF download path. The event contract MUST distinguish: (1) results_exported_json = handleExportResults trigger, (2) results_shared = handleShareResults trigger, (3) clinician_report_viewed = ClinicianReportRenderer tab/render event, (4) clinician_report_pdf_downloaded = DEFERRED (ReportsService disabled; not a live action). Labelling handleExportResults as 'clinician_report_downloaded' would be semantically wrong and must not occur.

changes.check: 2A — Source cross-ref: no paid conversion path present
changes.evidence: Grep across frontend/app/ for stripe, billing, subscription, payment, checkout: zero matches (consistent with preflight §3.3). paid_conversion cannot be instrumented as a product event today. The event contract must mark this as BLOCKED/proxy-only. Preflight §4 table: 'Paid conversion — Missing; not instrumentable in-app until commerce exists or proxy defined'.

changes.check: 2A — Source cross-ref: no segment dimension present

Questionnaire data exists as JSON in Analysis.questionnaire_data but no explicit segment enum in schema. Segment-split measurement is BLOCKED pending product definition + capture. Preflight §4: 'Segment split — Missing dimension; Bounded to high — needs product definition + capture'.

Prompt §SCOPE items 1-8 (lines 133-313) define exact required governance deliverables with explicit content requirements. §OUT OF SCOPE (lines 333-342) prohibits: event wiring, analytics SDK, BI/warehouse, experimentation, payment/billing, backend reasoning, generic logging, launch-posture reopening, fake precision on segmentation. No backend or application code changes permitted. §IMPLEMENTATION CONSTRAINTS 'Product behaviour untouched' (line 365): 'No application logic or instrumentation should be added in this sprint.'

Prompt scope item 8 (lines 299-313): validation must show (1) required artifacts created, (2) event vocabulary internally coherent, (3) privacy boundaries explicit, (4) no backend analytical behaviour changed. Bounded and proportionate to docs-dominant scope.

Five explicit STOP conditions (lines 385-395): (1) event definitions require unresolved strategy beyond agreed posture, (2) privacy/consent implications make contract misleading, (3) drift into instrumentation implementation, (4) inventing segment/paid-conversion logic product does not support, (5) only way to complete artifact is to overclaim metric readiness. Conditions 4 and 5 directly address the two confirmed gaps: no billing path (paid conversion blocked) and no segment dimension (must not be fabricated).

Prompt §IMPLEMENTATION CONSTRAINTS 'Product behaviour untouched' (line 365): no application logic or instrumentation added. No Intelligence Core components in scope. No analytical pipeline, InsightGraph, ranking/filtering, or output assembly touched. Emitted analytical reasoning unchanged.

§OUT OF SCOPE is comprehensive and directly addresses the known risk vectors: 'No event wiring or analytics SDK integration' (line 334) — cannot drift into WEDGE-METRICS-B territory; 'No fake precision on user segmentation or clinician carry-through' (line 341) — directly addresses the two unresolvable metrics. STOP condition 3 halts implementation drift. STOP condition 4 halts metric fabrication.
